Applied_Species.reason: The study directly investigates mdm-miR858 in apple fruit peel and apple calli.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The Summary states apple is Malus × domestica Borkh.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Apple peel experiments used 'Starkrimson Delicious' and apple callus experiments used 'Orin'.

Source_Species.reason: mdm-miR858 studied here is from apple.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Apple is identified as Malus × domestica in the paper.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The promoter and expression analyses were from 'Starkrimson Delicious' peel; the source variety is consistent with the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: mdm-miR858 is explicitly described as a miRNA/miR858.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results show mdm-miR858 expression is negatively correlated with PA accumulation during peel development and increases under light stress.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper reports apple peel developmental expression and induction under light stress.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S-driven mdm-miR858 constructs were used, and the native mdm-miR858 promoter was used in reporter assays.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The central metabolite regulated is proanthocyanidins (PAs).

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: PAs are discussed as flavonoids in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PA effects were measured in apple peel, apple calli, tobacco flowers, and Arabidopsis seeds.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Summary/Discussion state PAs have antioxidant properties and health benefits including antioxidation, anti-inflammation, and antitumour activity.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results directly show mdm-miR858 cleaves/represses MdMYB9/11/12, inhibits PA accumulation, and under light stress is associated with enhanced anthocyanin accumulation via suppression of the PA branch.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four listed findings are explicitly supported by Results figures/text: negative correlation with PAs, 5'-RACE/GUS/LUC evidence, reduced catechin/epicatechin/procyanidins/total PAs in multiple systems, and light-stress-associated reduction of PAs with enhanced anthocyanin.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results/Methods, including sRNA-seq, RT-qPCR, 5'-RACE, GUS assay, dual-luciferase, transgenic/overexpression analyses, HPLC, DMACA, and STTM in peel.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MdMYB9/11/12 are direct targets of the miRNA, but they are not proteins physically interacting with mdm-miR858 in the protein-protein sense; MdBBX22 interacts with the mdm-miR858 promoter, not with the miRNA itself.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Fruit developmental stages and continuous light treatment after bag removal at 120 DAFB are explicitly described.

Omics_Data.reason: sRNA-seq on Illumina HiSeq2500 is stated, but 'accession NA' is not a paper-derived value; no accession is provided in the text.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper supports post-transcriptional negative regulation of the PA branch, STTM858 increasing PAs and decreasing anthocyanins, and competition between PA and anthocyanin branches under light stress.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable application inference from the data, but it is not explicitly stated in the paper as a breeding recommendation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper shows increased mdm-miR858 lowers PAs and increases anthocyanin under light stress, while STTM858 has the opposite effect.

Applied_Species.reason: MdMYB9 regulator function was directly tested in apple and heterologous systems.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Starkrimson Delicious peel and Orin calli were used.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Source/developmental analyses used Starkrimson Delicious peel.

Regulator_Type.reason: MdMYB9 is an MYB transcription factor regulating PA genes.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is described within a PA-specific R2R3-MYB clade.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Expression in young peel correlates with PAs; transgenic calli overexpress MdMYB9.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Measured in apple peel during fruit development.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Measured in peel, calli, tobacco flowers and Arabidopsis seeds.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: PAs are described as beneficial antioxidants.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: MdMYB9 activates PA biosynthesis and raises PA accumulation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summary points are directly supported by 5'-RACE, transient assays, overexpression/silencing, and miR858 antagonism data.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: MdANR and MdLAR are the supported downstream structural targets discussed and tested with promoter activation assays.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The paper and cited prior work show MdMYB9 activates MdANR/MdLAR with bHLH cofactors; this paper functionally supports this in LUC assays.

Regulation_Mode.reason: MdMYB9 acts as a transcriptional activator of PA genes.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper states MdMYB9 can combine with MdbHLH33 and MdbHLH3 to promote structural gene expression.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: mdm-miR858-mediated post-transcriptional repression is directly demonstrated.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The wording matches the paper's evidence that MdMYB9 is a positive, redundant PA regulator.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: 5'-RACE, transient LUC, overexpression/silencing, HPLC and DMACA are all directly reported.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MdbHLH33/MdbHLH3 are supported partners, but mdm-miR858 is not a protein.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Fruit development stage was directly studied.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Functional redundancy with MdMYB11/12 is directly supported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible engineering inference, not directly stated/tested.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The study's tobacco data support an inverse relationship between enhanced PA accumulation and anthocyanin content.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The paper supports activation of MdLAR/MdANR with bHLH cofactors.

Regulation_Mode.reason: It functions as a transcriptional activator of the PA pathway.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper states MdMYB12 can combine with MdbHLH33/MdbHLH3.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: mdm-miR858 and light stress via MdBBX22 are supported upstream factors.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The miR858 target site importance and positive PA-regulator role are directly supported.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are present in the paper.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MdbHLH33/MdbHLH3 are supported partners, but mdm-miR858 is not a protein and MdBBX22 acts indirectly.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper supports that maintaining PA accumulation can reduce anthocyanin-based peel coloration under light stress.

Applied_Species.reason: MdBBX22 was directly tested in apple peel and calli.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Apple is identified as Malus × domestica.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Peel experiments used 'Starkrimson Delicious' and calli used 'Orin'.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Promoter screening/light-stress peel work was from 'Starkrimson Delicious'.

Regulator_Type.reason: MdBBX22 is treated as a transcription factor regulating mdm-miR858 transcription.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper identifies MdBBX22 as a BBX protein; BBX denotes a B-box transcription factor family.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: MdBBX22 expression increased under light stress and it was overexpressed in calli and peel assays.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Its increased expression in apple peel under light stress is shown.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S:MdBBX22 and mdm-miR858 promoter reporter assays were used.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: MdBBX22 is linked to changes in proanthocyanidins.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PA effects were measured in apple peel and calli.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: PAs are discussed as beneficial antioxidant flavonoids.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results directly show MdBBX22 activates mdm-miR858, suppresses PA accumulation, and is associated with enhanced anthocyanin synthesis under light stress.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four listed items are explicitly shown by Y1H screening, LUC/Y1H/EMSA/ChIP, callus overexpression analyses, and light-stress peel assays.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: The mdm-miR858 promoter is the direct target demonstrated by multiple binding assays.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: MdBBX22 activates mdm-miR858 and thereby indirectly reduces MdMYB9/11/12 and downstream MdLAR1/2 and MdANR1/2 expression in calli.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The paper supports control of flavonoid branch allocation between PA and anthocyanin synthesis under light stress.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Direct transcriptional activation via G-box binding in the mdm-miR858 promoter is shown by Y1H, EMSA, ChIP and LUC assays.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Light stress after bag removal is the tested upstream cue.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: This is a fair summary of the directly demonstrated light-responsive upstream role of MdBBX22 in reducing PAs and favoring anthocyanin accumulation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly used in the paper.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MdBBX22 binds the mdm-miR858 promoter G-box, but that is a DNA element not a protein; mdm-miR858 and MdMYB9/11/12 are downstream components rather than direct protein interaction partners shown here.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Continuous light stress after bag removal at 120 DAFB is explicitly described.

Other_Important_Info.reason: MdMYB12m partially alleviating MdBBX22-mediated inhibition is directly shown in Figure 7 and text.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Application to peel color improvement is an inference rather than a direct claim tested in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The Results show MdBBX22 overexpression decreases PAs while increasing anthocyanins.
